title: Different results when `-fno-split` is given due to blocking assignment

features.name: blocking assignment to reg in sequential always block
features.description: Code should include a blocking assignment (=) to a reg variable inside a sequential always block (@posedge clock)
features.snippet: always @(posedge clk) begin if ((-wire1)) reg0 <= wire1; else reg1 = wire0; end

features.name: mixed blocking and nonblocking assignments in same always block
features.description: Code should include both blocking (=) and non-blocking (<=) assignments to regs inside the same sequential always block
features.snippet: always @(posedge clk) begin if (wire0[1]) reg4 <= (~&wire2); if ((wire2 * wire2)) reg3 <= $unsigned(wire2); else reg3 <= reg4; end

features.name: multiple clocked always blocks with inter-block reg dependencies
features.description: Code should include multiple always @(posedge clk) blocks where one block reads a reg written by another block in the same clock cycle
features.snippet:
always @(posedge clk) begin if ((-wire1)) reg0 <= wire1; else reg1 = wire0; end
always @(posedge clk) begin reg2 <= {reg0}; end

features.name: concatenation with mixed reg and wire types
features.description: Code should include a concatenation operator combining reg variables and wire expressions
